ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Form used/assessment name
Notes: e.g., Self-Report, Parent Form, Short Form, Revised Version

ElementDescription: Timepoint/visit label
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime

ElementDescription: I can cope with whatever challenges come at me as I work to keep safe
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: I have to give up too much to keep safe.
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: I know what to do in response to threats to my safety
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: I have a good idea about what kinds of support for safety that I can get from people in my community (friends, family, neighbors, people in my faith community, etc.)
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: I know what my next steps are on the path to keeping safe.
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: Working to keep safe creates (or will create) new problems for me.
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: When something doesn't work to keep safe, I can try something else
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: I feel comfortable asking for help to keep safe.
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: When I think about keeping safe, I have a clear sense of my goals for the next few years
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: Working to keep safe creates (or will create) new problems for people I care about.
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: I feel confident in the decisions I make to keep safe.
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: I have a good idea about what kinds of support for safety I can get from community programs and services.
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true

ElementDescription: Community programs and services provide support I need to keep safe.
Notes: 1= Never true; 2= Sometimes true; 3= Half the time true; 4= Mostly true; 5= Always true
